---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: 299b412ca7c476c67b14d5f7c6eeb5bafc69c8bc
findings:
  - { id: judged-cf-1, kind: judged, text: "spec/draft-boards dc-1 and dc-3 make the address the distinction between the sealed record and drafts: unprefixed addresses serve the sealed-record view while every draft lives under a /b/{branch}/ path prefix, so which view a reader is in is carried entirely by the URL. Parent feature dc-2 rules that 'the status is the distinction, never the address'. The story declares an edge only against feature dc-3 (port retirement) — nothing supersedes or exempts feature dc-2's never-the-address principle, and a path-prefix distinction is arguably the port-pattern distinction relocated from the port to the path. (confidence 0.55)", disposition: no-conflict, note: "the /b/ prefix carries WHICH BRANCH'S TREE serves the render — a locator, unavoidable because one spec name can exist on N branches at once and the feature's own outcome mandates drafts 'backed by their own branch's working tree' at one address — while the VIEW (sealed vs authoring) is never carried by the URL: the same unchanged mode law computes it from branch state per instance (story ac-3; an unprefixed address on a design-branch serving checkout still renders authoring, dc-3 'semantics unchanged'). Feature dc-2's never-the-address law governs the directory's status distinction, which stays entirely status-keyed (directory-home dc-2); unlike the port pattern, /b/ needs no second process and no invented address — it is the one address, subdivided by tree", target_ref: spec/workbench-directory }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/draft-boards#dc-1, spec/draft-boards#dc-2, spec/draft-boards#dc-3, spec/draft-boards#dc-4, spec/workbench-directory#dc-1, spec/workbench-directory#dc-2, spec/workbench-directory#dc-3, spec/workbench-directory#dc-4, spec/workbench-directory#dc-5] }

judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"cf-1\",\"text\":\"spec/draft-boards dc-1 and dc-3 make the address the distinction between the sealed record and drafts: unprefixed addresses serve the sealed-record view while every draft lives under a /b/{branch}/ path prefix, so which view a reader is in is carried entirely by the URL. Parent feature dc-2 rules that 'the status is the distinction, never the address'. The story declares an edge only against feature dc-3 (port retirement) — nothing supersedes or exempts feature dc-2's never-the-address principle, and a path-prefix distinction is arguably the port-pattern distinction relocated from the port to the path.\",\"confidence\":0.55,\"target\":\"spec/workbench-directory\"}]}" }

---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-cf-1** [no-conflict]: spec/draft-boards dc-1 and dc-3 make the address the distinction between the sealed record and drafts: unprefixed addresses serve the sealed-record view while every draft lives under a /b/{branch}/ path prefix, so which view a reader is in is carried entirely by the URL. Parent feature dc-2 rules that 'the status is the distinction, never the address'. The story declares an edge only against feature dc-3 (port retirement) — nothing supersedes or exempts feature dc-2's never-the-address principle, and a path-prefix distinction is arguably the port-pattern distinction relocated from the port to the path. (confidence 0.55) — the /b/ prefix carries WHICH BRANCH'S TREE serves the render — a locator, unavoidable because one spec name can exist on N branches at once and the feature's own outcome mandates drafts 'backed by their own branch's working tree' at one address — while the VIEW (sealed vs authoring) is never carried by the URL: the same unchanged mode law computes it from branch state per instance (story ac-3; an unprefixed address on a design-branch serving checkout still renders authoring, dc-3 'semantics unchanged'). Feature dc-2's never-the-address law governs the directory's status distinction, which stays entirely status-keyed (directory-home dc-2); unlike the port pattern, /b/ needs no second process and no invented address — it is the one address, subdivided by tree
